category: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')
description: Contracts that create predictable, orderable state changes expose value to block builders and searchers (MEV). Sandwich attacks, backrunning, and liquidation front-running are examples. When a user's transaction has a predictable effect (e.g., large swap moving price), an attacker can place transactions before and after to extract value. SCWE-037 covers front-running; this weakness addresses the broader MEV surface—design choices that make extraction easy.
name: Extractable Value from Predictable Transaction Ordering
remediation: - Use private mempools or commit-reveal schemes for sensitive transactions. - Implement slippage protection (SCWE-090) and deadline parameters (SCWE-141). - Consider batch auctions, fair ordering, or MEV-aware design (e.g., CoW Protocol) where applicable.
